explanation: The LTL formula captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the system must remain in the TRANSITION state until the system is supported and the sensor data is good, at which point the system transitions to the NOMINAL state. The formula ensures that the transition happens only when the given conditions are met.

This LTL property captures the essence of the natural language statement by expressing that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  The use of 'until' ensures that the transition to NOMINAL happens once the conditions are met, while the use of 'eventually' allows for some time delay before the transition occurs.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot remains in the TRANSITION state until both the system is supported and the sensor data is good, at which point it transitions to the NOMINAL state.

The LTL specification ensures that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the system remains supported and sensor data remains good. The formula does not require that the autopilot stays in TRANSITION until sensor data is good and system is supported, since the system could be in other states before transitioning to NOMINAL. If the system is not supported or sensor data is bad, the formula does not impose any conditions on the autopilot state transitions.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state, but only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  It uses the 'until' operator (U) to express that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the conditions (supported and good) remain true. If either condition becomes false before the transition, the property will be false. The property does not specify a time limit for the transition to occur.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot will remain in the TRANSITION state until the system is supported and the sensor data is good, at which point it will transition to the NOMINAL state. The use of G ensures that this property will hold globally, i.e., at all times.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the transition to NOMINAL must occur only while the conditions are met. The use of G ensures the property holds globally, meaning this behavior should always happen.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to specify the condition under which the transition must occur. The antecedent of the U operator is the conjunction of the supported and good atomic propositions, representing that the system is healthy and the sensor data is good. The consequent is the atomic proposition state_is_NOMINAL, which indicates the autopilot's state transition to the NOMINAL state. The property ensures that the transition occurs only under the specified conditions and does not impose any constraints on how long the transition takes.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the 'until' operator (U) to express the condition that the state remains in TRANSITION until both 'supported' and 'good' are true, at which point the state transitions to NOMINAL.

This LTL specification captures the requirement that when the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the supported and good conditions are true. If the supported and good conditions ever become false, the property will not be satisfied even if the autopilot eventually reaches the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property states that, globally, if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, then it will eventually change to the NOMINAL state.  It directly captures the requirement that the transition to NOMINAL must happen when the conditions are met.

This LTL property states that eventually, when the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.  It uses the atomic propositions to represent the system state and sensor data. The use of F ensures that the transition will happen eventually, not necessarily immediately. The conjunction of "supported" and "good" ensures both conditions must be met.

This LTL specification captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The use of the "U" (until) operator ensures that the transition to NOMINAL occurs only after the condition of supported system and good sensor data is met. The use of the & operator ensures that both supported and good conditions must be true for the transition to occur.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the system must remain in the TRANSITION state until the conditions (supported & good) are true and then transition to the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to specify that the transition to NOMINAL must occur before the system is unsupported or the sensor data becomes bad. The formula (supported & good) -> F state_is_NOMINAL checks that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the state will become NOMINAL. The antecedent of the implication ensures that the transition only happens under the specified conditions. The implication states that if the supported and good conditions hold, then eventually the state_is_NOMINAL must hold. The formula G (state_is_TRANSITION -> ( (supported & good) -> F state_is_NOMINAL) ) ensures this happens globally, i.e., in all states.

This LTL specification captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The formula uses the 'until' operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot remains in the TRANSITION state until both the supported and good conditions are met, at which point it transitions to the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot must remain in the TRANSITION state until both the supported and good conditions are true, at which point it must eventually transition to the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the transition to NOMINAL must occur while both supported and good conditions are true. The property ensures that the autopilot does not transition to the NOMINAL state unless both system support and good sensor data are present. It directly addresses the natural language description's requirement.

The LTL specification captures the requirement that when the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the atomic propositions "supported", "good", "state_is_TRANSITION", and "state_is_NOMINAL" to represent the relevant conditions and states. The "->" operator implies that if the antecedent (supported & good) is true, then the consequent (F(state_is_NOMINAL)) must eventually be true after the antecedent is true. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally, meaning that at every point in time, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, a transition to NOMINAL will eventually occur.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually change its state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the state will remain in TRANSITION until both the system is supported and the sensor data is good, at which point it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state. The use of G ensures that the property holds globally and always.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to specify that the transition to NOMINAL must occur while the system remains supported and the sensor data remains good. If the system becomes unsupported or the sensor data becomes bad, the transition to NOMINAL is not required.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The use of the "until" operator ensures that the transition to NOMINAL happens only after the supported and good conditions are met. The property does not imply the autopilot will remain in the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the system must remain in the TRANSITION state until both the supported and good conditions are met, at which point it transitions to the NOMINAL state. The use of G ensures that this behavior occurs globally (always).

The LTL formula captures the requirement that when the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the system remains in the TRANSITION state until the conditions are met and the transition to NOMINAL occurs. The formula ensures that the transition happens eventually once the conditions are satisfied and it does not specify exactly when or how quickly the transition has to happen.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The property uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the transition to NOMINAL must occur before the condition of 'supported' and 'good' becomes false. The use of the implication operator ensures that this transition only happens when the condition is met and does not require any specific timing between them.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the transition to NOMINAL state will happen when the system is supported and the sensor data is good. The property ensures that the autopilot will not transition to the NOMINAL state if either the system is not supported or sensor data is not good.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually change its state to NOMINAL from TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the state will remain in TRANSITION until the conditions are met. Then, it uses the "next" operator (X) to indicate that the state transition to NOMINAL happens in the next step after the condition is met.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The use of the "until" operator ensures that the transition to NOMINAL only happens once the condition of supported system and good sensor data is met. The conjunction of supported and good ensures both conditions must be true.

This LTL property states that if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.  It uses the atomic propositions to represent the states and conditions of the system and ensures that the transition happens when the specified conditions are met.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The use of the "until" operator ensures that the transition to NOMINAL only happens when both supported and good are true. The implication (->) states that if the system is in TRANSITION and both supported and good are true, then it will eventually reach NOMINAL state.

This LTL specification captures the essence of the natural language statement.  It states that whenever the system is supported ('supported') and the sensor data is good ('good'), the autopilot will eventually change its state from TRANSITION ('state_is_TRANSITION') to NOMINAL ('state_is_NOMINAL'). The use of 'U' (until) ensures that the transition to NOMINAL happens after the conditions are met.  No other LTL property is provided, hence no comparison.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and the sensor data is good and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, then eventually the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state. This captures the essence of the natural language input by specifying the conditions under which the state transition must occur.

It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the state remains TRANSITION until the conditions for change to NOMINAL are met. The formula ensures that the state change happens eventually after the supporting conditions become true.

It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the supported and good conditions hold. The property does not capture the requirement that the transition should happen immediately upon the conditions being met; instead, it allows for some delay between the conditions being met and the transition happening.

This LTL property captures the requirement that when the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot remains in the TRANSITION state until both "supported" and "good" are true, at which point it must eventually reach the NOMINAL state.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually change its state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the state remains in TRANSITION until the state becomes NOMINAL. The antecedent, (supported & good), must be true for the consequent to be true. It directly addresses the natural language prompt.

This LTL specification captures the essence of the natural language statement by expressing the condition under which a state transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL is expected.  It uses the atomic propositions "supported" and "good" to represent the system's health and sensor data quality, respectively, and "state_is_TRANSITION" and "state_is_NOMINAL" to reflect the autopilot's state. The formula ensures that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, eventually the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state.  This addresses the "shall" requirement by specifying the eventual outcome. There is no other possible LTL specification that can be more accurate for this statement.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The formula uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the system must be supported and the sensor data must be good until the state transitions to NOMINAL. The property ensures that the transition to NOMINAL only occurs under these conditions. It does not specify a time limit for the transition, only that the transition to NOMINAL will happen if the condition remains true.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL must occur before a point in time where either system support is lost or sensor data becomes bad. The use of implies ensures that the transition happens only when the conditions are met. It does not capture any timing constraints.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (represented by the atomic propositions "supported" and "good"), the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the supported and good conditions remain true. The use of G ensures that this property holds globally throughout the system's operation.

It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot will eventually reach the NOMINAL state if the supported and good conditions are met. The property ensures that the transition to the NOMINAL state occurs under specific system conditions and doesn't happen otherwise.

The formula uses the "until" operator (U) to specify that the transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL must occur eventually after the condition (supported & good) becomes true. The formula prioritizes capturing the essence of the transition rather than other aspects like state duration.

It uses the atomic propositions to represent the states and conditions. The use of G ensures the property holds globally across all time steps. The implication (->) operator is used to express the conditional requirement.

The LTL specification (supported & good) -> F state_is_NOMINAL ensures that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state.  This captures the essence of the natural language statement by stating the condition under which the state transition should happen.  It does not, however, capture the requirement that the transition must happen from the TRANSITION state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state once the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the transition to NOMINAL must happen while the system remains supported and the sensor data remains good. The property ensures the autopilot does not transition to NOMINAL under any other circumstances.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must eventually transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state when the system is supported and sensor data is good.  The use of the 'until' operator ensures that the transition to the NOMINAL state happens only when the conditions are met, and not otherwise. The formula is structured to check for the supported and good conditions continuously until the autopilot reaches the NOMINAL state. If the supported and good conditions are not met, the formula does not force a transition to the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to specify that the transition to the NOMINAL state must happen before the supported or good conditions are no longer true. The property also uses the implication operator (->) to ensure that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the transition to NOMINAL will occur.

This LTL specification captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.  The formula ensures that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state. The use of the "until" operator ensures that the transition happens before the condition of system support and good sensor data is no longer met.
